patch:
commit 9927a06f74bb48e1e5a53fb686301c71f0dec46a
Author: Ricky Zhou <ricky@rzhou.org>
Date:   Sat Dec 18 18:49:17 2021 +0100

    [AA] Handle callbr instructions in alias analysis
    
    Before this change, AAResults::getModRefInfo() was missing a case for
    callbr instructions (asm goto), which may read/write memory. In PR52735,
    this led to a miscompile where a load was incorrect eliminated.
    
    Add this missing case, as well as an assert verifying that all
    memory-accessing instructions are handled properly.
    
    Fixes #52735.
    
    Differential Revision: https://reviews.llvm.org/D115992

diff --git a/llvm/lib/Analysis/AliasAnalysis.cpp b/llvm/lib/Analysis/AliasAnalysis.cpp
index af63692fb7b8..49199060786c 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Analysis/AliasAnalysis.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Analysis/AliasAnalysis.cpp
@@ -696,14 +696,16 @@ ModRefInfo AAResults::getModRefInfo(const Instruction *I,
   case Instruction::AtomicRMW:
     return getModRefInfo((const AtomicRMWInst *)I, Loc, AAQIP);
   case Instruction::Call:
-    return getModRefInfo((const CallInst *)I, Loc, AAQIP);
+  case Instruction::CallBr:
   case Instruction::Invoke:
-    return getModRefInfo((const InvokeInst *)I, Loc, AAQIP);
+    return getModRefInfo((const CallBase *)I, Loc, AAQIP);
   case Instruction::CatchPad:
     return getModRefInfo((const CatchPadInst *)I, Loc, AAQIP);
   case Instruction::CatchRet:
     return getModRefInfo((const CatchReturnInst *)I, Loc, AAQIP);
   default:
+    assert(!I->mayReadOrWriteMemory() &&
+           "Unhandled memory access instruction!");
     return ModRefInfo::NoModRef;
   }
 }

tests.test_body:
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define dso_local i32 @foo() {
entry:
  %v = alloca i32, align 4
  %0 = bitcast ptr %v to ptr
  callbr void asm "movl $$1, $0", "=*m,X,~{dirflag},~{fpsr},~{flags}"(ptr nonnull %v, ptr blockaddress(@foo, %out))
          to label %asm.fallthrough [label %out]

asm.fallthrough:                                  ; preds = %entry
  br label %out

out:                                              ; preds = %asm.fallthrough, %entry
  %1 = load i32, ptr %v, align 4
  ret i32 %1
}
tests.source_program:
target triple = "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"

define dso_local i32 @foo() {
entry:
  %v = alloca i32, align 4
  %0 = bitcast ptr %v to ptr
  callbr void asm "movl $$1, $0", "=*m,X,~{dirflag},~{fpsr},~{flags}"(ptr nonnull %v, ptr blockaddress(@foo, %out))
          to label %asm.fallthrough [label %out]

asm.fallthrough:                                  ; preds = %entry
  br label %out

out:                                              ; preds = %asm.fallthrough, %entry
  %1 = load i32, ptr %v, align 4
  ret i32 %1
}

issue.title: [GVN] miscompile of `callbr`
issue.body:
Consider the following code:
```llvm
%struct.percpu_list_node = type { i64, %struct.percpu_list_node* }

define %struct.percpu_list_node* @this_cpu_list_pop() {
  %head = alloca %struct.percpu_list_node*, i32 0, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:
  callbr void asm sideeffect "", "i,i"(i8* blockaddress(@this_cpu_list_pop, %cleanup), i8* blockaddress(@this_cpu_list_pop, %cleanup.thread19))
          to label %cleanup.thread [label %cleanup, label %cleanup.thread19]

cleanup.thread:
  %x = load %struct.percpu_list_node*, %struct.percpu_list_node** %head, align 8
  br label %return

cleanup:
  br label %for.cond

cleanup.thread19:
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %cleanup.thread, %cleanup.thread19
  %retval.118 = phi %struct.percpu_list_node* [ %x, %cleanup.thread ], [ null, %cleanup.thread19 ]
  ret %struct.percpu_list_node* %retval.118
}
```
```sh
$ opt -passes=gvn reduced.ll -S -o -
```
```llvm
source_filename = "reduced.ll"

%struct.percpu_list_node = type { i64, %struct.percpu_list_node* }

define %struct.percpu_list_node* @this_cpu_list_pop() {
  %head = alloca %struct.percpu_list_node*, i32 0, align 8
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %cleanup, %0
  callbr void asm sideeffect "", "i,i"(i8* blockaddress(@this_cpu_list_pop, %cleanup), i8* blockaddress(@this_cpu_list_pop, %cleanup.thread19))
          to label %cleanup.thread [label %cleanup, label %cleanup.thread19]

cleanup.thread:                                   ; preds = %for.cond
  br label %return

cleanup:                                          ; preds = %for.cond
  br label %for.cond

cleanup.thread19:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %cleanup.thread19, %cleanup.thread
  ret %struct.percpu_list_node* null
}
```
That doesn't look quite right to me; we're now unconditionally returning `null`. cc @gwelymernans @jyknight @efriedma-quic @eefriedman 

Reported-by: @compudj
issue.author: nickdesaulniers

comments.author: efriedma-quic
comments.body: Doesn't look like a miscompile to me.  %x is a load from an uninitialized variable, i.e. undef.  phi of undef and null is null.

comments.author: nickdesaulniers
comments.body:
I think is a more demonstrative test case:
```c
int* foo (void) {
  int *head;
  asm goto(""::"m"(head):"memory":null);
  return head;
null:
  return (void*)0;
}
```
```sh
$ gcc -O2 /tmp/x.c -S -o -
```
```asm
foo:
.LFB0:
        .cfi_startproc
        movq    -8(%rsp), %rax
        ret
        .p2align 4,,10
        .p2align 3
.L3:
.L2:
        xorl    %eax, %eax
        ret
```
```sh
$ clang -O2 /tmp/x.c -S -o -
```
```asm
foo:                                    # @foo
        .cfi_startproc
# %bb.0:                                # %entry
        #APP
        #NO_APP
.Ltmp0:                                 # Block address taken
# %bb.1:                                # %cleanup
        xorl    %eax, %eax
        retq
```

comments.author: duk-37
comments.body:
[see here](https://godbolt.org/z/exscrvqqG)

`-mllvm -enable-gvn-memdep=false` mitigates the issue.

What's seems to be happening is one of two things:
1. GVN is not properly querying MemDep for `callbr` instructions
2. MemDep isn't properly handling `callbr`.

comments.author: rickyz
comments.body:
Took a look at this, and it looks like AA was missing a case for callbr instructions here:
https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/591371f7df3cee0429c85f2aeb4a848df8888386/llvm/lib/Analysis/AliasAnalysis.cpp#L685-L708

Sent out https://reviews.llvm.org/D115992, which fixes the test case in https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/52735#issuecomment-996017004 for me.

comments.author: nickdesaulniers
comments.body: Thanks @rickyz and @duk-37 ! Reopening to request a backport into the 13.0.1 release.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: Yeah, should be fine to backport.

comments.author: compudj
compudj
